title: arctostaphylos stanfordiana subsp. hispidula
intro: Arctostaphylos stanfordiana subsp. hispidula, also known as the Stanford manzanita, is a shrub species native to California, belonging to the family Ericaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which contribute to its health benefits. The leaves and stems of the Stanford manzanita can be used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which may help to soothe digestive issues, boost the immune system, and promote wound healing. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antioxidant and antiviral properties, making it a valuable resource in traditional and modern herbal medicine.
